reveh discussion" of such plans
should be repressed by "self-re-
spectaing, stable government" Roose-
welt ought to turn a volley Of intunc-
t1ons from his capon-lined Taft upon
this meeting, for it is not only going
to discuss government and ask for
things beneficial to the most intelli-
gent body of laborers in the land, but
1T is even going to discuss the sub
ject of amalgamating with the Ameri-
can Federation of Labor, on the clear
ly avowed ground that 1.-The organiza-
ton should make itself felt in poll
tics"


This last is most pernicious. One
can almost already see the acres of
goose pimples of horror breaking out
over the bodies of ex-Judges Taft,
Herrick and others.


Down with public ownership, for
the people will make themselves felt
in politics! The unions will vot solid
ly on some issue Of interest to them!


Violence and lawlessness are never
justifiable. But l believe that strike
and boycott, legitimately conducted.
are justifiable. / see them everywhere
resorted to. in one form or another,
by merchants and manufacturers who
would scorn to join a trades union
and who would laugh at the idea that
their tricks of the trade" boycotts
and determinations were as repre-
hensible as any resorted to by organ
lied labor.


The picture of Debs in convict
garb. painted by this same Taft. who
has his eyes on the presidency. in
functions against union talking.
whistling, making Of faces, or any
thing else asked by corporations of
prejudiced If not corrupt courts,
troops at beck and call of private
concerns, the organization Of an army
of low-down but high paid worthless
scamps as professional strike break
ers have taken from unionism all
there was of efficacy or legitimacy in
strike or boycott.


In Gods name, what other recourse
than to make themselves felt in poll
tics have the unions, or the people at
large?


1t is we'll that letter carriers or
ganize. 1t is well that employees un-
der public ownership organize. For
politics? Yes, for political strength
and solidity in behalf of the rights of
common humanity and in behalf Of
the right.
